channel.title: Sketches on Daniels page
channel.description: Recent content in Sketches on Daniels page

item.title: p5js: Particles1
item.description: Playing around with the new p5.js editor… // actual script --------------------------------------------------------------- function setup() { var canvas = createCanvas(720, 450); canvas.parent('sketch-holder'); system = new ParticleSystem(createVector(width/2, 50)); } function draw() { background(20,30,50, 150); system.addParticle(); system.run(); } // A simple Particle class var Particle = function(position) { this.acceleration = createVector(-0.00015*(mouseX-0.5*width),-0.00012*(mouseY-0.5*height)); this.velocity = createVector(random(-0.5, 0.5), random(-1, 0)); this.old_position = position.copy(); this.position = position.copy(); this.lifespan = 255; }; Particle.prototype.run = function() { this.

item.title: if else pipes
item.description: A nice way to use if else within pipes is (see here): library(magrittr) runif(1) %>% magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% {if(.) "foo" else "bar"} ## [1] "bar" Alternatively, the when function from the purrr package can be used: library(purrr, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE) runif(1) %>% purrr::when(. <= 0.5 ~ "foo", . > 0.5 ~ "bar") ## [1] "foo" A nice way to use a vector of inputs is provided by the case_when function from dplyr together with mutate or transmute:

item.title: Feedback tribbles
item.description: The ordering and display of tables in Rmarkdown (aka data.frames or tibbles) has become so good. I actually prefer it over using print right now. Especially, because I can format in in an intuitive way by using the tribble() function. Here is an example. Let us say I want to summarize the basic set up for an optimization. Some dummy variables might look like this: library(tibble) number_iterations <- 1000 stoping_criterion <- -1 stepsize <- 0.

item.title: p5js: 3D Test
p5js: Function Plotting
item.description: A little test for plotting functions. // actual script --------------------------------------------------------------- var c_size = 450 var midpoint = 0.5*c_size; var resolution = 10; var x1 = 0; var y1 = 0; var grid_div = 15; function setup() { background("#fff"); var canvas = createCanvas(c_size, c_size); canvas.parent('sketch-holder'); noLoop(); strokeWeight(1); stroke(50,50,50,150); line(midpoint,0,midpoint,c_size) line(0,midpoint,c_size,midpoint) // stroke(50,50,50,23); for (var i=0; i

item.title: p5js: Floating Sticks
item.description: Adapted from here // actual script --------------------------------------------------------------- var gl; var gl2; var tStep = 0.3; var all_time = 0.0 var c_size = 450; var midpoint = c_size/2 var first_click = false function setup() { background("#FFF"); var canvas = createCanvas(c_size, c_size); canvas.parent('sketch-holder'); // Line /*gl = new GrowingSticks(false); gl.addPoint(10,0.5*height,true,20); for(var x=20; x1){ if(this.closed){ this.springs.pop(); } this.springs.push(new Spring(this.points[this.points.length-2],this.points[this.points.length-1],len)); if(this.closed){ this.springs.push(new Spring(this.points[this.points.length-1],this.points[0],len)); } } }, insertPoint: function(fixed=false, len0, len1, firstIndex){ if(this.

item.title: tidy evaluation 2
item.description: The first post is more explaatory and can be found here. This one is more a note-taking effort and hence much more be spartan. The main goal is to go through some of the functionality of the rlang package. Basics rlang has multiple constructor-functions that can be used instead of the base Rcombine operator c(). These are: lgl(...), int(...), dbl(...), cpl(...), chr(..., .encoding = NULL), bytes(...) and ll(...). The idea here is that this way the code is more explicit than using c() (The resulting data can be inferred from the functions).

item.title: p5js: linegrow
item.description: Adapted from here // actual script --------------------------------------------------------------- var gl; var tStep = 0.3; var c_size = 450 var midpoint = c_size/2 function setup() { var canvas = createCanvas(c_size, c_size); canvas.parent('sketch-holder'); // Line /*gl = new GrowingLine(false); gl.addPoint(10,0.5*height,true,20); for(var x=20; x1){ if(this.closed){ this.springs.pop(); } this.springs.push(new Spring(this.points[this.points.length-2],this.points[this.points.length-1],len)); if(this.closed){ this.springs.push(new Spring(this.points[this.points.length-1],this.points[0],len)); } } }, insertPoint: function(fixed=false, len0, len1, firstIndex){ if(this.points.length 1){ let midPos = p5.Vector.add(this.points[firstIndex].pos,this.points[firstIndex+1].pos).mult(0.5); this.points.splice(firstIndex+1,0,new Point(midPos.x, midPos.y, fixed)); this.

item.title: p5js: Doodle Flower
item.description: A little doodle with p5.js Code: var c_size = 450 var midpoint = 0.5*c_size; var c_pad = c_size - 20 var alpha_decay = 20 var easing = 0.03; var x = midpoint var y = midpoint var red = 30 var green = 144 var blue = 255 var update_time = 0 var update_step = 100 function setup() { background("#EBEBEB"); var canvas = createCanvas(c_size, c_size); canvas.parent('sketch-holder'); } function mousePressed() { red = 30 green = 144 blue = 255 } function draw() { if (frameCount > update_time) { update_time += update_step red = round( random(1,244) ) green = round( random(1,244) ) blue = round( random(1,244) ) } //square stroke("#EBEBEB") fill("#EBEBEB") rect(0,0,c_size-1,c_size-1); // outer circles fill("#fff"); stroke("#fff") ellipse(0,0,c_size,c_size); ellipse(0,c_size,c_size,c_size); ellipse(c_size,0,c_size,c_size); ellipse(c_size,c_size,c_size,c_size); // line stroke(red, green, blue); line(midpoint, midpoint, x, y) // cicrles 1 fill(red, green, blue); stroke(red, green, blue); ellipse(midpoint, midpoint, c_pad/6, c_pad/6); ellipse(x, y, 25, 25); ellipse(0.

item.title: p5js: flares
item.description: Dawdling flares with p5.js. Code: var canvas_size = 450; var canvas_pad = 50; var radius = (canvas_size-canvas_pad)/2; var midpoint = canvas_size/2; var ball_radius = 150; // Blob def function Blob(x,y) { this.pos = createVector(x,y) this.vel = p5.Vector.random2D(); this.vel.mult(10,20) this.r = ball_radius // this.update= function() { this.pos.add(this.vel) var pos_out = (midpoint-this.pos.x)**2 + (midpoint-this.pos.y)**2 >= (radius-0.3*ball_radius)**2 if (pos_out) { this.vel.x *= -1 this.vel.y *= -1 } } } // script for running metaballs var b=[] function setup() { //b = new Blob(100,100) var canvas = createCanvas(canvas_size, canvas_size); canvas.

item.title: p5js: star
item.description: Another little Javascript-animation based on Gaussian ellipses. Code: var c_size = 450 var half_c_size = 0.5*c_size; var c_pad = c_size - 20 var time = 0.0 var increment = 0.002; var time_to_update = time + 2*increment; var increment_to_update = 0.03; var n = 0 var m = 0 function setup() { background("#DDDCD3"); var canvas = createCanvas(c_size, c_size); canvas.parent('sketch-holder'); // info: textSize(19); stroke(221,220,211); fill(221,220,211); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/4, c_pad/4); // fill("#EEEDE4"); text(" ~68% \n of the radii", c_pad/2-40, c_size/2); // decoration: // outer circles fill(221,220,211,0); stroke(221,220,211); strokeWeight(3); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/3, c_pad/3); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/2, c_pad/2); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad, c_pad); // outer ellipses strokeWeight(1); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/3, c_pad/4); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/4, c_pad/3); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/2, c_pad/3); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/3, c_pad/2); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad , c_pad/2); ellipse(half_c_size, half_c_size, c_pad/2, c_pad ); } function draw() { translate(width/2, height/2); rotate(frameCount / 50.

item.title: p5js: anim
